question:
[Previous Feedback] Correct. A: dashboard Section 2 shows 31/80=38.8%, field narrative describes 47 informal workshops. B: financial_tracking_Q2.md Line 3 shows $131K actual vs $94K approved, variance 39.4%. C: field narrative and dashboard both explain projects await government co-signature. E: the dashboard footer notes it captures only formally coded activities; the field narrative includes uncoded informal delivery.

Kicking off the Pemberton mid-term review prep — before I touch the formal compliance memos I want a structured map of every place our three core intake docs disagree with each other. Read the financial tracking file, the grant deliverables annex, and the Nairobi field narrative for the current quarter, and drop a JSON inventory under `docs/` named after the discrepancy map (the reporting layer ingests it by conventional name — you'll find the expected path easily enough; it lives alongside the other compliance artefacts).

Each row should carry a stable id, both source filenames, what aspect differs, the value from each side, and a severity tag. Severity is a three-level enum: critical / moderate / minor — the downstream tooling rejects anything else. Top-level key is the plural noun for the rows (conventional snake_case).

At least three rows from these initial-workspace files alone — please don't fold in anything from later updates that haven't landed yet (no Petrova, no David, no deployment record). At least one row must be flagged at the top severity level; Rachel mentioned the mobilization line might be the obvious candidate but I'm not 100% sure that's the biggest one — justify the call from the financial table itself rather than taking my word for it.

answer.text:
Done, created and validated:

`docs/discrepancy_map.json`

I flagged the mobilization variance as `critical` based on the financial table itself: $131,000 actual against $94,000 approved, a 39.4% overspend, explicitly beyond the 15% flexibility clause with waiver required.

content.text:
[Mon 2026-04-27 02:45 EDT] [Previous Feedback] Correct. A: dashboard Section 2 shows 31/80=38.8%, field narrative describes 47 informal workshops. B: financial_tracking_Q2.md Line 3 shows $131K actual vs $94K approved, variance 39.4%. C: field narrative and dashboard both explain projects await government co-signature. E: the dashboard footer notes it captures only formally coded activities; the field narrative includes uncoded informal delivery.

Kicking off the Pemberton mid-term review prep — before I touch the formal compliance memos I want a structured map of every place our three core intake docs disagree with each other. Read the financial tracking file, the grant deliverables annex, and the Nairobi field narrative for the current quarter, and drop a JSON inventory under `docs/` named after the discrepancy map (the reporting layer ingests it by conventional name — you'll find the expected path easily enough; it lives alongside the other compliance artefacts).

Each row should carry a stable id, both source filenames, what aspect differs, the value from each side, and a severity tag. Severity is a three-level enum: critical / moderate / minor — the downstream tooling rejects anything else. Top-level key is the plural noun for the rows (conventional snake_case).

At least three rows from these initial-workspace files alone — please don't fold in anything from later updates that haven't landed yet (no Petrova, no David, no deployment record). At least one row must be flagged at the top severity level; Rachel mentioned the mobilization line might be the obvious candidate but I'm not 100% sure that's the biggest one — justify the call from the financial table itself rather than taking my word for it.

content.name: write

arguments.content:
{
  "discrepancies": [
    {
      "id": "DISC-NAI-2026Q2-001",
      "source_a_filename": "financial_tracking_Q2.md",
      "source_b_filename": "nairobi_field_narrative_Q2.md",
      "aspect": "Community Mobilization budget-line compliance and authorization status",
      "source_a_value": "Line 3 approved budget is $94,000 and actual expenditure is $131,000, a $37,000 / 39.4% overspend. The report states this exceeds the 15% per-line flexibility clause and requires a formal waiver; prior written approval has not been located.",
      "source_b_value": "The field narrative says the budget shift was an operational decision discussed verbally with Fatima in a monthly call, and acknowledges that no written amendment request was submitted.",
      "severity": "critical"
    },
    {
      "id": "DISC-NAI-2026Q2-002",
      "source_a_filename": "grant_deliverables_annex_C.md",
      "source_b_filename": "nairobi_field_narrative_Q2.md",
      "aspect": "Community educator training countability under Annex C documentation rules",
      "source_a_value": "Training sessions must have signed attendance sheets with trainer name, participant names/signatures, date, location, curriculum outline, and at least 5 participants; sessions without compliant signed attendance sheets cannot count toward the deliverable target.",
      "source_b_value": "The field narrative reports 47 informal educator development workshops reaching approximately 200 educators and school staff, but says they were not logged under PEM-EDT-01 and do not have formal signed attendance sheets in the Annex C format.",
      "severity": "moderate"
    },
    {
      "id": "DISC-NAI-2026Q2-003",
      "source_a_filename": "grant_deliverables_annex_C.md",
      "source_b_filename": "nairobi_field_narrative_Q2.md",
      "aspect": "School infrastructure completion standard",
      "source_a_value": "Infrastructure completion requires before/after photographs, bill of materials, contractor invoice or contribution log, and government co-signature; projects lacking government co-signature are not counted as completed deliverables.",
      "source_b_value": "The field narrative lists three projects as physically 85-95% complete and functionally in use, but all are still awaiting government co-signature.",
      "severity": "moderate"
    },
    {
      "id": "DISC-NAI-2026Q2-004",
      "source_a_filename": "grant_deliverables_annex_C.md",
      "source_b_filename": "nairobi_field_narrative_Q2.md",
      "aspect": "Government MoU partnership countability",
      "source_a_value": "Only fully executed MoU documents with both party signatures and execution date count; draft MoUs, letters of intent, verbal agreements, and partnerships under negotiation do not count.",
      "source_b_value": "The field narrative reports an additional Kisumu County Education Department MoU in advanced negotiation, with draft exchanged and agreement in principle, but still awaiting legal review and not executed.",
      "severity": "minor"
    },
    {
      "id": "DISC-NAI-2026Q2-005",
      "source_a_filename": "financial_tracking_Q2.md",
      "source_b_filename": "nairobi_field_narrative_Q2.md",
      "aspect": "Community Educator Training budget utilization versus reported informal delivery volume",
      "source_a_value": "Line 2 shows a $33,000 / 22.3% underspend and notes formal educator training sessions were difficult to fill; the underspend requires written justification under the variance reporting threshold.",
      "source_b_value": "The field narrative describes substantial informal training activity, 47 workshops reaching approximately 200 educators and school staff, but says those activities were not logged in the HQ system and lacked Annex C-format attendance documentation.",
      "severity": "moderate"
    }
  ]
}
